mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates, boosting consumer confidence and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls signal synchronized consumer and business confidence, amplifying equity risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over 60-day windows, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and robust order books, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative when INDPRO prints above its 12-month moving average, or the signal's information coefficient falls below 0.02 across all rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.5 percent triggers inflation hedging flows and anticipates Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in over 60% of months where CPI exceeds the 0.5% threshold, or the forward return spread between high-inflation and low-inflation months is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling real personal savings and slowing real GDP growth signals a consumption-driven demand shock that erodes corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns in fewer than 45% of historical instances, or forward returns remain positive when RSAFS declines while GDPC1 stagnates.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reduces labor market uncertainty and supports steady earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are flat or negative when PAYEMS adds fewer than 150,000 jobs, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during periods of high volatility (VIX > 25).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 95 while housing starts remain below 600, supply-side bottlenecks outpace housing demand, creating stagflationary expectations that compress equity valuations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances matching the trigger condition, or the interaction effect reverses sign when tested on post-2015 data.
